---
title: Getting Started (Vue)
---

import CodeWithPreview from '@components/astro/CodeWithPreview.astro';
import { LinkButton, Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

export const example = String.raw`
<script setup>
  import { ref } from 'vue'
  import 'hover-tilt';
  const msg = ref('Tilt me!')
</script>

<template>
  <hover-tilt>
    <div class="card">
      <h1>{{ msg }}</h1>
    </div>
  </hover-tilt>

  <input v-model="msg" placeholder="Type a message..." />
</template>
`;

#### Using the Web Component in Vue

Here's an example using the `<hover-tilt>` web component in your Vue Single File Component and bind the content to a ref.

<Aside type="caution" title="Vue Component Resolution Warning">
  Vue may trigger warnings related to `Failed to resolve component: 'hover-tilt'`. See the [Vue documentation on how to
  fix this warning](https://vuejs.org/guide/extras/web-components#skipping-component-resolution).
</Aside>

<CodeWithPreview
  lang="vue"
  title="HoverTiltDemo.vue"
  code={example}
>

<p class="p-4 pb-8 text-center">
  <LinkButton
    icon="external"
    target="_blank"
    rel="noopener noreferrer"
    href="https://play.vuejs.org/#"
  >{`View a Vue live demo here`}</LinkButton>
</p>

</CodeWithPreview>

#### TypeScript Support

For TypeScript projects with Volar, you can also import `hover-tilt/vue` to enable template type-checking and IntelliSense at the app level.

```ts
import { createApp } from 'vue';
import App from './App.vue';

import 'hover-tilt'; // defines the custom element
import 'hover-tilt/vue'; // pulls in GlobalComponents typings (type-only)

createApp(App).mount('#app');
```

<Aside type="note" title="Note">
  `hover-tilt/vue` is a TypeScript-only augmentation and requires a bundler (Vite/Webpack). It won't work in browser
  playgrounds like Vue Playground.
</Aside>
